text: This paper examines the perceptions and attitudes of middle-aged individuals toward agritourism, offering insights into their specific behaviors and expectations when vacationing in rural settings. The study identifies the primary motivations driving this demographic to choose agritourism and explores the factors that influence their selection of specific destinations. Furthermore, the research analyzes the information channels tourists use to discover rural offers, their preferred leisure activities, and their expectations regarding service diversification. The findings provide a foundation for improving agritourism offerings to better align with the evolving demands of middle-aged travelers.
